title: Build active-users filter
description:
## Build an active-users filter

Export a function `activeFilter(minAge)` that returns a MongoDB filter object selecting users where:

- `status` equals `'active'`
- `age` is greater-than-or-equal to `minAge` (use the `$gte` operator)

Return the filter as a plain JS object — do not call any DB driver.

title: Group-by-category pipeline
description:
## Group-by-category aggregation

Export a function `countByCategory()` that returns a MongoDB aggregation pipeline (an array) which:

1. Matches documents where `inStock` is `true`.
2. Groups by `category`, accumulating a `count` field with `$sum: 1`.
3. Sorts results by `count` descending.

Return only the pipeline; do not execute anything.

title: Find by multiple statuses
description:
## Find by multiple statuses

Export a function `findStatuses(statusArray)` that returns a MongoDB filter object.

It should match documents where the `status` field matches any of the values provided in the `statusArray`. Use the `$in` operator.

title: Match array elements
description:
## Match array elements

Export a function `findHighMathScores()` that returns a MongoDB filter.

We have a `scores` array containing objects like `{ subject: 'math', score: 85 }`. The filter should match documents where the `scores` array contains AT LEAST ONE element that has `subject` equal to `'math'` AND `score` greater than or equal to `90`.

Use the `$elemMatch` operator.

title: Complex OR logic
description:
## Complex OR logic

Export a function `premiumOrAdmin()` that returns a filter object.

It should match documents where EITHER:
- `role` is `'admin'`
- `subscription` is `'premium'`

Use the top-level `$or` operator.

title: Regex Search
description:
## Regex Search

Export a function `searchName(keyword)` that returns a MongoDB filter object to search the `name` field.

Use the `$regex` operator with the provided keyword, and add the `$options: 'i'` flag to make the search case-insensitive.

title: Check field existence
description:
## Check field existence

Export a function `validPhoneFilter()` that returns a MongoDB filter.

It should match documents where the `phoneNumber` field BOTH exists (using `$exists: true`) AND is of type string (using `$type: 'string'`).

title: Set and Increment
description:
## Set and Increment

Export a function `updateProfile()` that returns a MongoDB update document.

It should:
- Increment the `views` field by `1` (use `$inc`).
- Set the `lastUpdated` field to the string `'today'` (use `$set`).

title: Push to array
description:
## Push to array

Export a function `addTag(tag)` that returns an update document.

It should append the provided `tag` to the `tags` array using the `$push` operator.

title: Remove from array
description:
## Remove from array

Export a function `removeBadReviews()` that returns an update document.

It should remove ALL elements from the `reviews` array where the `rating` property is less than `3`. Use the `$pull` operator combined with `$lt`.

title: Upsert Logic
description:
## Upsert Logic

Export a function `upsertUser(email)` that returns an update document designed to be used with an upsert operation.

It should:
- `$set` the `email` field to the provided email.
- `$setOnInsert` the `createdAt` field to the string `'now'` (this ensures it only writes the date if a new document is created).

title: Reshape documents
description:
## Reshape documents

Export a function `formatUsers()` that returns an aggregation pipeline (array) with a single `$project` stage.

The `$project` stage should:
- Exclude the `_id` field (set to `0`).
- Include the `email` field (set to `1`).
- Create a new field `fullName` using the `$concat` array operator to combine `firstName`, a space `' '`, and `lastName` (e.g. `{ $concat: ['$firstName', ' ', '$lastName'] }`).

title: Pagination Pipeline
description:
## Pagination Pipeline

Export a function `paginatePipeline(page, limit)` that returns an aggregation pipeline array.

Calculate the `skipAmount` as `(page - 1) * limit`. 
The pipeline should have two stages in this exact order:
1. `$skip` by the `skipAmount`.
2. `$limit` by the `limit` parameter.

title: Unwind and Group
description:
## Unwind and Group

Export a function `tagFrequency()` that returns a pipeline to count how many times each tag appears across all documents.

1. `$unwind` the `tags` array (use the string `'$tags'`).
2. `$group` by the tag name (set `_id` to `'$tags'`), and accumulate a `count` field using `{ $sum: 1 }`.

title: Join Collections
description:
## Join Collections

Export a function `joinUsers()` that returns a single-stage aggregation pipeline to perform a left outer join.

Use the `$lookup` stage to join the current collection with the `users` collection. 
- `from`: `'users'`
- `localField`: `'authorId'`
- `foreignField`: `'_id'`
- `as`: `'authorDetails'`

title: Calculate Totals
description:
## Calculate Totals

Export a function `calculateTotals()` that returns a single-stage pipeline.

Use the `$addFields` (or `$set`) stage to create a new field called `totalPrice`. The value should be the result of multiplying the `$price` and `$quantity` fields using the `$multiply` array operator.

title: Group and Filter (Having)
description:
## Group and Filter

Export a function `frequentBuyers()` that returns a 2-stage pipeline.

1. `$group` by `'$userId'` and accumulate a `totalOrders` field using `{ $sum: 1 }`.
2. `$match` only the grouped results where `totalOrders` is greater than or equal to `5` (use `$gte`).
